title: Chiliadenus antiatlanticus
intro: Chiliadenus antiatlanticus, also known as Moroccan orpine, is a plant species belonging to the family Lamiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions including wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Chiliadenus antiatlanticus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and diterpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Chiliadenus antiatlanticus is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Chiliadenus antiatlanticus is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Atlantic islands. The classification of Chiliadenus antiatlanticus includes its kingdom as Plantae, its phylum as Magnoliophyta, and its class as Magnoliopsida. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
